name: Parchment Wrapped Chicken
recipeIngredient: 2 Whole chicken breasts, skinned and boned
6 Green onions with tops, cut into 2-inch lengths
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut eight 8-inch squares of parchment paper.
2. Cut chicken into thin, narrow strips, about 3 inches long.
3. In a large bowl, combine soy sauce, ginger juice, and sugar. Stir in the chicken and let stand for 30 minutes.
4. Pour boiling water over bean sprouts; let stand one minute. Drain and cool under cold water, then drain well.
5. Thoroughly toss the chicken mixture with bean sprouts, green onions, and walnuts.
6. Place about half a cup of chicken mixture in the center of each parchment square.
7. Fold the bottom point of the parchment over the filling, creasing just below the filling. Fold the point over and under the filling.
8. Fold the side points over the filling, overlapping slightly, and crease the paper to hold the folds.
9. Fold the remaining corner down so it extends below the bottom of the bundle; tuck this point between the folded sides. Crease the paper to hold the folds.
10. Repeat with the remaining parchment squares (they will look like small envelopes).
11. Place the bundles seam side down in a single layer on a steamer rack.
12. Set the rack in a large pot or wok of boiling water, ensuring the water level does not reach the bundles. Cover and steam for about 7 minutes or until the chicken is tender. Serve immediately.
To make ginger juice: Peel fresh ginger root and squeeze through a garlic press.
